item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kinda short but I still like it

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The heartrate is reducing BY 1bpm every 30 seconds (aka reducing by 2 beats every minute) until it reaches a final rate of 1bpm. So if your original heartrate was 60, it would take only about half an hour to end up at 1bpm but it would be going down at the same rate the entire time, not drop suddenly the entire amount. If you're a person with an unusually fast BPM of 120, it would take about an hour to get down to 1.

How that interacts with the natural fear response (of an increased heartrate) once it's been noted by sensors that their heartrate is plummeting, we'll never know 😆

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. bpm is abrreviation of beat per minute

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Footnote 2 - Is "1bpm every 30 seconds" deliberate? 1 beat per minute every 30 seconds?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I liked this a lot better before I went to the comments and realized it was EOD. Don't get me wrong, I love me some EOD, but the evocative Holmesian mystery is my jam. Still a +1 for the sake of the enjoyment it gave me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It took me a minute to realize why this is EoD, but man the symbolism here is really superb. I couldn't figure out exactly what this meant but the minute I understood it, it all made sense.

I have to say that this is a well-written abstract piece of art. The way you tied things in here are really quite good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Open if you want some spoiler
- Close

Hello, roget.

First, thanks for commenting. and pardon for my odd syntax.

Second, this is the result of experimenting about writing in a very tight time for me. And this suffers because of it.

Third, I agree that the description is thin. And the reason of that it's a combination of trying to make it subtle1 and because I don't write in English, I have to think how to write it, translate, correcting odd syntax and asking someone to see the draft.

Fourth, the novel descriptions all share a common theme and that is the omission of a certain word. Also, the Lady is symbolic.

Fifth, I could have put a crosslink, but I wanted to let the readers figure it themselves. About the scientist, for us it behaves badly, in Foundationverse it behaves badly too but for different reasons and in his mind he has the purest intentions.

After jamcon maybe I will change some things.

Footnotes
1. Maybe glossing information that I considered "fluff" has harmed the subtlety.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very thin and feels like a glorified anomalous object. It feels like large portions of the story are missing, it glosses over almost every detail in the description and the only substantive portion are the novel descriptions which themselves feel detached from one another and lacking in depth or continuity beyond the theme of a mad scientist behaving badly.

-1

item.title: Our Beloved Lady is...
item.content:encoded:
Well, another day, another SCP for jamcon. This was harder than I thought.

Thanks to Uncle Nicolini for seeing it and correcting odd syntax.

Have a good read for a scp which definitely does not happen in a popular setting.
